name: Error Handling Policy Extractor
short_description: Infers per-layer and per-module error-handling policies by classifying usage of throws, Result<T,E>, Publisher.failure, Optional, and silent-log patterns across Swift and Kotlin codebases, producing a policy graph that distinguishes propagation style by architectural layer.
tech_stack: SwiftSyntax (swift-syntax) — AST visitor detects: try/throw/catch sites, Result<_, Error> return types, AnyPublisher<_, Error> signatures, optional-return-on-failure patterns
semgrep — multi-language pattern rules for: silent-log patterns, error swallowing, Kotlin runCatching + fold/getOrNull vs getOrThrow usage
ast-grep — structural matching for Kotlin sealed class Error hierarchies, Swift enum conforming to Error, and Swift typed throws (Swift 6 feature) signatures
detekt (custom RuleSet) — Kotlin-side: catches EmptyCatchBlock, detects Result<T,E>.getOrNull() used where propagation expected, flags runCatching chains
SourceKit-LSP — type resolution to determine actual Error type propagated (typed vs untyped)
LLM (optional, heuristic upgrade) — only when determining intent: distinguishes intentional boundary error-swallowing from accidental swallowing
entity_model: Produces :ErrorPolicy nodes (layer∈{domain|data|presentation|network|ui}, platform∈{iOS|Android|shared}, style∈{throws|Result|PublisherFailure|Optional|SilentLog|Mixed}, typed_errors:bool, confidence_pct) with :GOVERNS edge to :Module. :ErrorBoundary nodes (location:file+line, boundary_kind∈{catch-all|Result-to-throws-conversion|Publisher-catch}, upstream_style, downstream_style) representing style transitions. :VIOLATES edge from :File to :ErrorPolicy when a file uses a style inconsistent with its module's declared policy. Faceted axes: semantic-kind=Policy, domain-concept=ErrorHandling, capability=:Audits.
storage_format: graph-node — policy nodes are persistent per-module facts stable across ingests; :ErrorBoundary nodes carry valid_from/valid_to for tracking when boundary was introduced or removed

prior_art.name: Interleaving static analysis and LLM prompting for error specification inference (Springer STTT 2025)
prior_art.description: Hybrid approach: static analyzer generates candidate error specs, LLM refines them.

prior_art.name: semgrep taint-mode and error-swallowing rules
prior_art.description: Semgrep supports structural pattern matching for empty catch blocks, ignored Result values, and silent-log patterns.

prior_art.name: detekt EmptyCatchBlock and similar rules
prior_art.description: detekt ships SwallowedException, TooGenericExceptionCaught, EmptyCatchBlock, and PrintStackTrace rules covering Kotlin error-handling anti-patterns.

prior_art.name: Swift typed throws (SE-0413, Swift 6)
prior_art.description: Swift 6 typed throws changes error propagation semantics.

prior_art.name: CodeQL 2.25.0 Swift 6.2.4 support
prior_art.description: CodeQL's Swift data-flow engine understands throws/Result propagation at the type level.

prior_art.name: Combine error handling patterns (Kodeco, avanderlee.com)
prior_art.description: Documents Publisher.failure, mapError, catch, replaceError patterns.

consumer_query_patterns.label: What error style does each module use by layer?
consumer_query_patterns.cypher: MATCH (ep:ErrorPolicy)-[:GOVERNS]->(m:Module) RETURN m.name, ep.layer, ep.style, ep.confidence_pct ORDER BY m.name, ep.layer

consumer_query_patterns.label: Find all error-swallowing violations in the domain layer
consumer_query_patterns.cypher: MATCH (f:File)-[:VIOLATES]->(ep:ErrorPolicy {layer:'domain', style:'SilentLog'}) RETURN f.path, ep.module ORDER BY f.path

consumer_query_patterns.label: Where do error-handling style transitions (boundaries) occur?
consumer_query_patterns.cypher: MATCH (eb:ErrorBoundary) RETURN eb.location, eb.upstream_style, eb.downstream_style, eb.boundary_kind

consumer_query_patterns.label: Which modules mix throws and Result in the same layer (Mixed style)?

graphiti_episode_type: EntityNode — :ErrorPolicy nodes carry valid_from and valid_to (when pattern changes). :ErrorBoundary nodes are EpisodicNode when representing a single conversion event at a specific commit.
ingest_perf_est: [uncertain] Full ingest estimated 10–20 min on 350K LOC combined. Incremental on changed files: ~60–120 s.
failure_modes: Silent logging via custom Logger wrapper not matching known log-call patterns
Legitimate catch-all UI boundary handlers classified as violations
Swift typed throws: pre-Swift-6 heuristics misclassify typed throws as untyped
Combine vs async/await migration: Mixed style may be correct in-progress
Generated stub code using throws everywhere
KMP shared code error style may legitimately differ
open_questions: Model Result→throws conversion as boundary node or edge property?
Swift 6 typed throws: how to treat 'throws Never'?
Silent-log detection requires per-project logger-library config
Does schema need :ErrorType node separate from :ErrorPolicy?
Hard dependency on extractor #6 (Coding Convention) for layer assignment
adversarial_resistance: medium — basic empty-catch and silent-log patterns are reliably detected. Adversarial code hiding error swallowing inside abstraction layers will evade AST detection.
regulatory_sensitivity: low — error messages occasionally contain user-identifying data. Silent-log classification may surface log calls containing sensitive crypto material; output nodes should not store log message content.
